summary: Resolve a lawful, lowest-cost path to reach a property owner (free public sources before paid skip-trace), keeping outreach_allowed:false until DNC/consent is verified.

files.role: resolveContactRoute(): pure engine that picks the legal contact path for a node/goal given available keys and channels.
Lower-level contact routing primitives used across the contact stack.
Turns a property Thinga substrate payload into executable ContactRouteEngine args + a route-pack Thinga + KG entities/edges/citations.
complianceCheck(): gates every contact; outreach_allowed stays false until DNC/consent checked.
skiptraceDecision(): decides whether paid skip-trace is justified before spending.
Persists route-pack KG entities/edges/citations to wholesale_kg.
Built report of route-pack KG projection.

routes.summary: Resolves a contact route for a node or property_id; reports key presence (batchdata_api_key) without exposing the value.

tests.role: Vertical smoke test across the contact-route path.
Tests for the property contact-route pack.
Compliance gate tests (outreach blocked until DNC/consent).

tables.name: kg_entities
tables.role: Route-pack KG nodes (owner/contact path) persisted from packs.

tables.name: kg_citations
tables.role: Source citations backing each route-pack fact.

tables.name: properties
tables.role: Source of the node fields (address, owner_name, owner_mailing) the route is resolved from.

docs.role: Contact route engine behavior and legal-path policy.

audit_findings.finding: Operator views should expose source trust and terms risk per lead/property; the property -> owner -> contact route -> compliance -> buyer -> offer path is named as the graph the system should make visible.
Contact route resolution is part of the runtime capability map for reaching owners lawfully.

next_actions.action: Make the chosen contact route and its compliance status a first-class, inspectable graph edge on each property rather than a one-shot API response.
Surface per-route source trust / terms risk so operators see why a path is allowed or blocked.
